title: pluchea arabica
intro: Pluchea arabica, also known as Arabian camphor or Khaki, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae that is native to various parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pluchea arabica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve its leaves, which are used to make teas, infusions, or essential oils for topical application.
